situation: How does your team currently handle [specific process] — is it mostly manual, partially automated, or fully systematized?
What tools does your team use for [area] today, and how long have they been in place?
How many people on your team touch [process] on a daily basis?
What does your current tech stack look like for [function]?
How do you measure success for [department/function] right now?
Walk me through what happens when [trigger event] occurs — who does what?
How is your team structured around [function]?
What's your typical timeline from [start point] to [end point]?
Where does [data/information] live today — one system or scattered?
How do you currently onboard new [team members/customers]?
problem: What's the most frustrating part of your current [process]?
Where do things typically break down or slow down?
What would your team say is the biggest bottleneck in [area]?
How often do you find yourself manually fixing things that should be automated?
What happens when [common failure point] occurs?
Are there tasks your team spends time on that feel like they shouldn't require human effort?
When was the last time [current tool] failed you in a critical moment?
How much time does your team spend on [low-value activity] each week?
What workarounds has your team built to compensate for gaps in [current solution]?
If you could eliminate one recurring headache in [area], what would it be?
How confident are you in the data coming out of your current [system]?
What visibility do you have into [key metric] right now?
How well does your current setup scale as you add [customers/users/data]?
What's the biggest surprise or blind spot you've encountered with [current approach]?
How easy is it for a new team member to get up to speed on [process]?
implication: If [problem] continues for another 6 months, what impact does that have on your [revenue/growth]?
How does [bottleneck] affect your ability to close deals / serve customers faster?
When [failure point] happens, what's the downstream effect on your team's productivity?
What does the lost time on [manual process] cost you in terms of deals not pursued?
If your team is spending X hours/week on [low-value task], what higher-impact work isn't getting done?
How does [data quality issue] affect the decisions your leadership team makes?
When [current tool] falls short, does that ever impact the customer experience?
If scaling [process] requires headcount, what does that do to your unit economics?
How does [problem] affect your ability to retain top performers on the team?
What happens to your competitive position if [competitor] solves this faster than you?
If [onboarding delay] continues, how does that impact your time-to-value for new customers?
How much pipeline leaks out because [visibility gap] means deals don't get followed up in time?
If you can't fix [problem] before [upcoming event/deadline], what are the consequences?
What's the compound effect of [problem] over the next year if nothing changes?
How does [issue] affect your team's confidence in the numbers they report to leadership?
When [breakdown] occurs, how long does it take to recover, and what's the real cost of that downtime?
If [manual process] introduces errors, what's the risk to [compliance/customer trust/revenue]?
How does [limitation] affect your ability to experiment with new [strategies/channels/products]?
What opportunities are you leaving on the table because [current approach] can't handle [scenario]?
If your top salesperson leaves tomorrow, how much institutional knowledge walks out the door because [knowledge gap]?
need_payoff: If you could cut [time on process] in half, what would your team do with that freed-up capacity?
How would it impact your revenue if [conversion rate/speed] improved by even 20%?
What would it mean for your team if [painful process] just worked automatically?
If you had real-time visibility into [key metric], how would that change your weekly decisions?
How valuable would it be to onboard new [customers/team members] in [time] instead of [current time]?
If [problem] was eliminated, what would that mean for your ability to hit your [quarterly/annual] targets?
What would your leadership say if you could show them [specific improvement] in [timeframe]?
How much would it be worth to never have to worry about [recurring issue] again?
If scaling didn't require adding headcount, how would that change your growth plans?
What would it mean for your competitive position if you were the fastest in your space at [capability]?
